commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: And account that the longsuffering of our Lord is salvation; even as our beloved brother Paul also according to the wisdom given unto him has written unto you; As also our most dear brother, Paul. hath written to you. He seems to mean in his epistle to the Hebrews or converted Jews, (Chap. ) where he says: yet a little while. And he that is to come, will come, and will not delay. In which are some things hard to understand, especially by unlearned, ignorant people, unstable, inconstant, not well grounded in faith, and which they wrest, as they do also the other scriptures, by their private interpretations, to their own perdition.

commentaries.author: Polycarp of Smyrna
commentaries.content: These things, brethren, I write to you concerning righteousness, not because I take anything upon myself, but because ye have invited me to do so. For neither I, nor any other such one, can come up to the wisdom
